figures.caption: Table 4. Phase 2 best combinatorial prompts. ∆: mAP@0.5 gain over “a flower” baseline. Emoji rows (†) show the best prompt with an appended emoji token.

Table 1. Systematic prompt axis definitions for one-factor-at-a-time (OFAT) analysis for cowpea flowers. Baseline values represent the control group used as a fixed anchor during single-axis perturbations. The final prompt is constructed as: [Grammar] [Color] [Taxonomy] [Anatomy] [Phenology] [Negation] [Emoji].

Table 2. Phase 2 combinatorial generation strategy. Base combinations are generated programmatically from the three sweeps below; negation and emoji variants are then appended to the top-ranked results. Axes marked with ⋆ use the best value identified in Phase 1 OFAT analysis.

Figure 2. Qualitative comparison of baseline (left half: "a flower") versus best discovered usable prompt (right half) across all four VFMs on a representative real image (median-ranked by F1). Green boxes denote ground-truth annotations; red boxes denote predictions above the model’s F1-maximizing confidence threshold from synthetic benchmarking. YOLO World and OWLv2 have the largest visible performance gain, while Grounding DINO and SAM3 also improve by F1, but less obviously.

Figure 1. Phase 1 OFAT analysis: change in mAP@0.5 relative to each model’s baseline prompt (dashed circle at ∆ = 0). Each plot shows one prompt axis; vertices represent perturbation values from Table 1. Legend: YOLO World, SAM3, Grounding DINO, OWLv2.

Table 3. Phase 1 Benchmark Results: Best Single-Prompt Performance. ∆ represents the mAP@0.5 improvement over the generic baseline prompt (“a flower”). Baseline YOLOv11 achieves 0.763 mAP@0.5 on real.
